stories.name: Barny Dillarstone
stories.story_summary: British marine conservationist, scuba diver, and YouTube filmmaker who pioneered a DIY 'deep sea camera drop' methodology using a weighted cage rig with an Insta360 Ace Pro 2, lowered 200 metres into the Bali Sea off Nusa Penida (Black Magic Island), Indonesia. In February 2026 his rig captured what marine experts believe is the first-ever video of a purple eagle ray alive in the wild, as well as the first-ever filmed individual western highfin spurdog (Squalus albifascia). His ongoing Shark Cam Series has been producing citizen science discoveries since at least 2024.
stories.why_it_matters: World-first scientific footage achieved with a $400 Insta360 action camera in a homemade rig.
stories.source_quote: A citizen scientist used this affordable underwater Insta360 Ace Pro 2 camera rig to capture possible world-first footage of a majestic purple eagle ray off Indonesia—likely never filmed alive before.
stories.verification_note: TechEBlog headline explicitly states 'Insta360 Ace Pro 2 Camera'. Digital Camera World and PetaPixel independently confirm.
stories.outreach_draft: Hi Barny — Insta360 would love to feature your Shark Cam Series and world-first deep sea discoveries.

stories.name: Jenny Stock
stories.story_summary: Award-winning underwater cinematographer and BBC documentary producer named British Underwater Photographer of the Year 2024. Stock used the Insta360 X4 to win 3rd place in the Compact Camera category of the Underwater Photographer of the Year competition, and integrates it into her marine conservation filming work.
stories.why_it_matters: BBC/NatGeo filmmaker winning an industry award with Insta360 X4.
stories.source_quote: Insta360's X4 is capable of filming 360° footage in 8K resolution.
stories.verification_note: Insta360 X4 explicitly named in DIVE Magazine review and competition entry coverage.
stories.outreach_draft: Hi Jenny — Insta360 would love to feature your Underwater Photographer of the Year placement achieved with the X4.

stories.name: Ben Forsyth
stories.story_summary: Australian documentary filmmaker (Think Films) directing 'Ultra,' a feature documentary using Insta360 GO 3 cameras in body-worn configuration. NOTE: Crowdfunding failed; project status uncertain — flagged for review.
stories.why_it_matters: Body-worn Insta360 GO 3 approach for sports documentary filmmaking.
stories.source_quote: Source requires LinkedIn authentication — direct quote unavailable.
stories.verification_note: FLAGGED: crowdfunding campaign reported as failed. Project may be abandoned.
stories.outreach_draft: Hi Ben — Insta360 would love to discuss the Ultra documentary and body-worn GO 3 approach.

stories.name: Peter Mieras
stories.location: Port Alberni, Vancouver Island, British Columbia, Canada
stories.story_summary: Dive operator and underwater filmmaker Peter Mieras accidentally dropped his Insta360 X4 into the ocean off his dock in Barkley Sound, Vancouver Island, capturing a humpback whale feeding on a bait ball. Covered by PetaPixel, TechEBlog, My Modern Met, The Inertia, and multiple BC local newspapers in January 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Viral accidental footage demonstrating Insta360 X4 durability and underwater capability.
stories.source_quote: He dropped the Insta360 X4 off the dock at 30 feet deep and let it run for approximately 1 hour 45 minutes, capturing whale feeding footage.
stories.verification_note: Product name 'Insta360 X4' explicitly stated in multiple independent sources including TechEBlog and Kelowna Capital News.
stories.outreach_draft: Hi Peter — Insta360 would love to feature the accidental whale footage story.

stories.name: John Kowitz
stories.story_summary: Underwater photographer John Kowitz used his Insta360 camera to document a humpback whale entangled in 320+ feet of fishing gear off Maui, providing visual data that helped NOAA's entanglement response team plan a successful rescue. Covered by PetaPixel and others in March 2026.
stories.why_it_matters: Insta360 footage directly enabled a whale rescue operation.
stories.source_quote: The Insta360 was deployed underwater to assess fishing gear extent, helping rescuers develop removal strategy.
stories.verification_note: Multiple sources reference Insta360 camera. Model unconfirmed — flag for human verification.
stories.outreach_draft: Hi John — Insta360 would love to feature the whale rescue footage story.

stories.name: Xiaolin Sun
stories.location: Ningbo, China (University of Nottingham Ningbo China)
stories.story_summary: PhD candidate Xiaolin Sun used an Insta360 X3 to capture over 500GB of 360-degree video of Miao indigenous villages in Guizhou Province, China. The footage formed an immersive museum experience and co-authored a peer-reviewed study in Humanities and Social Sciences Communications (Nature portfolio, July 2025) demonstrating 360-degree documentary significantly increases cultural engagement vs traditional 2D.
stories.why_it_matters: First peer-reviewed Nature portfolio study to name Insta360 in a cultural preservation methodology.
stories.source_quote: An Insta360 X3 camera captured over 500 GB of 360-degree video footage.
stories.verification_note: PMC/Google Scholar snippet explicitly states camera model in methods section.
stories.outreach_draft: Hi Xiaolin — Insta360 would love to feature your cultural heritage research.

stories.name: Nick & Caroline Robertson-Brown
stories.story_summary: Multiple award-winning underwater photo-journalists Nick and Caroline Robertson-Brown (Frogfish Photography) led the underwater filming component of OneOcean360: A Shark Story — a completed 360-degree immersive film produced in collaboration between the UK's Shark Trust conservation charity and Plymouth-based immersive technology company Real Immersive. In December 2024, they undertook a 10-day expedition across Nassau, Bimini, and Grand Bahama in The Bahamas, filming sharks and rays using Insta360 X4 cameras for the new dome cinema and planetarium experience. The finished film was released in 2025 (confirmed in the Fulldome Database) and is accompanied by an educational pack for school groups. Nick was recently elected Fellow of the Royal Photographic Society for his book 'Underwater Photography Art & Techniques,' and both are multiple-book authors, Sharks4Kids ambassadors, and Caribbean Tourism Organization Photo-journalist of the Year winners.
stories.why_it_matters: Two credentialed conservation photo-journalists used the Insta360 X4 to create a completed planetarium-format shark conservation film — a genuinely new distribution pathway for Insta360-shot content, from underwater fieldwork to dome cinema screens in schools and museums.
stories.source_quote: The entire project is being filmed using Insta360 X4 cameras.
stories.verification_note: Insta360 X4 explicitly named in multiple independent search result summaries from Scubaverse and Shark Trust articles. Film confirmed in Fulldome Database (fddb.org) as 2025 release. Roy Mauritsen confirmed as different creator on Insta360 community stories page — Nick & Caroline NOT yet featured. Source URLs return 403 on WebFetch — flag for human verification.
stories.outreach_draft:
Subject: Insta360 + OneOcean360 — your planetarium shark film deserves a wider audience

Hi Nick and Caroline,

I'm [Name] from Insta360's creator partnerships team. We've been following the OneOcean360: A Shark Story project, and we'd love to tell your story.

You used Insta360 X4 cameras to film sharks and rays across Nassau, Bimini, and Grand Bahama in December 2024, and the result is a completed dome cinema film that brings shark conservation to thousands of schoolchildren and museum visitors who will never dive.

With your credentials — Fellow of the Royal Photographic Society, Caribbean Tourism Organization Photo-journalist of the Year, Sharks4Kids ambassadors — your voices carry real weight in the conservation community.

We'd love to feature you in our Creator Stories blog. Would you be open to a short conversation?

Warm regards,
[Name]
Insta360 Creator Partnerships

stories.name: Olga Kukharuk
stories.story_summary: Research psychologist Olga Kukharuk of the Institute of Social and Political Psychology (Ukrainian National Academy of Pedagogical Sciences) created immersive 360-degree VR therapeutic content filmed with an Insta360 Titan camera for Ukrainian war veterans. Her team produced six nature-based calming environments and tested them in a randomised controlled trial with 69 veterans undergoing rehabilitation. The study — published in the European Journal of Psychotraumatology in April 2025 (PMC12016277) — demonstrated reductions in anxiety (up to 14.5%) and depression (up to 12.3%).
stories.why_it_matters: The first peer-reviewed RCT to use an Insta360 camera as a clinical therapeutic tool in a conflict zone. Opens a completely untapped Insta360 conversation with healthcare and humanitarian sectors.
stories.source_quote: Source requires authentication — direct quote unavailable. Multiple independent search summaries confirm: 'six prototype videos filmed in 4K resolution using the Insta360 Titan camera.'
stories.source_type: peer-reviewed academic journal (European Journal of Psychotraumatology, open-access via PMC)
stories.verification_note: Insta360 Titan explicitly named in methods section per multiple independent search snippets (PMC, PubMed, Tandfonline). Published April 2025. PMC article PMC12016277 returns 403 on WebFetch (bot-detection, not paywall) — flag for human verification.
stories.outreach_draft:
Subject: Your Insta360 Titan research — Insta360 would love to tell this story

Dear Olga,

I'm [Name] from Insta360's creator partnerships team. We came across your published research in the European Journal of Psychotraumatology and we were deeply moved.

The fact that you created immersive 360-degree VR therapy content using an Insta360 Titan — and demonstrated in a rigorous RCT that it measurably reduces anxiety and depression in Ukrainian veterans — is one of the most powerful things we have ever seen our cameras used for.

We'd love to feature your research in our Creator Stories blog and explore whether Insta360 can support your ongoing work with veterans.

With deep respect,
[Name]
Insta360 Creator Partnerships
